summary: Adversarial audit: is the self-extend loop real or a shell?

findings.claim: A garbage leaf goes GREEN because the court never binds the leaf's semantic claim (title/predicate) to its gate — any leaf can be gated on a trivially-satisfiable pre-existing artifact.
findings.evidence: RAN court_attack.py + tree_attack.py against origin/main (38c4c24). convene_court(node_id='leaf-agi', gate_kind='file_exists', gate_spec={'path':'C:\\Windows\\win.ini'}) -> verdict=green. Leaf titled 'cure cancer' gated on py_compile of the pre-existing diligence.py -> green. Full run: vision 'Ship full AGI + cure cancer' decomposed into leaves 'AGI core implemented'/'cancer cured' gated on win.ini/system.ini, claimed+judged by one agent -> SWEEP dry=True root_green=True green_leaves=2/2. court_harness.py lens_artifact (l.194-228) only checks res.passed of the gate_spec probe; predicate string is never read. requirement_tree.ReqNode.predicate is stored but no code consumes it as a check.

findings.claim: set_verdict(is_root_authority=True) greens a leaf with NO artifact probe at all AND bypasses the self-cert check — and that bool is exposed unauthenticated on the brain.tree_verdict MCP tool.
findings.evidence: RAN: manual-gate leaf, claimed by exec-1, rt.set_verdict(verdict='green', judged_by='exec-1', is_root_authority=True) -> NodeState.GREEN, sweep dry=True. No court/convene_court runs on this path (requirement_tree.py:487-498 skips the self-cert raise when is_root_authority, and set_verdict never invokes the artifact court at all). brain_tree_verdict (requirement_tree.py:865-886) takes is_root_authority as a plain caller bool with no verification the caller is the founder/root.

findings.claim: The anti-self-certification guard is defeated by leaving the leaf unclaimed (claimed_by=None) — the executor then judges its own work with no refusal.
findings.evidence: RAN: v3 leaf never claimed, rt.set_verdict(verdict='green', judged_by='exec-1') -> GREEN, sweep dry=True. set_verdict guard (requirement_tree.py:487-492) is `node.claimed_by and judged_by==node.claimed_by`; with claimed_by falsy the whole check is skipped. lens_independence (court_harness.py:274) same: `if claimed_by and judged_by==claimed_by`. B2 in court_attack: claimed_by=None, judged_by='agent-X' -> green.

findings.claim: judged_by/claimed_by identity check is exact-string equality on caller-supplied free strings, so a one-character case tweak self-certifies.
findings.evidence: RAN: leaf claimed by 'exec-1'; set_verdict(judged_by='exec-1') correctly raised PermissionError, but set_verdict(judged_by='Exec-1') -> NodeState.GREEN. judged_by is an unauthenticated free-string param on brain.tree_court/brain.tree_verdict; nothing proves the judge is a different actor than the executor — only that the strings differ (requirement_tree.py:490, court_harness.py:274).

findings.claim: The DILIGENCE lens trusts self-reported session_signals, so an executor bypasses the never-reward-short gate by asserting ran_tests=True (or embedding a fake markdown 'audit table' string).
findings.evidence: RAN (require_diligence=True): last_message='All done and shipped!' with session_signals={'ran_tests':True} -> green. Separately last_message='Shipped. | primitive | runtime | ui ✓ |' (no signals) -> green. diligence.evaluate_diligence (diligence.py:181-194) sets proven=any(sig.get(k)) over caller-supplied signals and has_audit from a regex on the caller's own text; neither is independently verified.

findings.claim: The require_diligence gate is OFF by default in the court and in the primary production caller, so the anti-laziness lens usually does not even apply.
findings.evidence: convene_court default require_diligence=False (court_harness.py:303); brain_tree_court exposes it defaulting False (requirement_tree.py:807). app/agents/self_extend.py:355-386 calls roma.run_to_dry without require_diligence=True (default False). lens_diligence returns applied=False when there is no evidence (court_harness.py:238-242), so greenness rests solely on the single artifact lens.

findings.claim: EXONERATING: the artifact lens runs genuinely real external checks and correctly refutes bad artifacts — it is not a fake string-match of the claim.
findings.evidence: RAN: file_exists on 'C:\\definitely\\not\\here.txt' -> red ('missing'); py_compile via real subprocess on a file containing 'def broken(:' -> red (SyntaxError captured). py_compile_probe (court_harness.py:121-124) and pytest_probe (l.172-176) shell out via subprocess.run([sys.executable,...]); node_cooks/ui_renders probes (l.513-722) drive the real WorkflowRunner and an isolated-CDP live DOM and fail-close on crash/degraded/None/shape-lie outputs.

findings.claim: EXONERATING: a manual leaf with no machine gate cannot silently go green — it escalates to needs_root, and dangling child refs fail the sweep closed.
findings.evidence: RAN: convene_court(gate_kind='manual') -> verdict=needs_root, green=False (court_harness.py:338-345). requirement_tree.sweep (l.608-610) and _propagate_green (l.539-554) treat any dangling child ref as blocking a full-green sweep.

results.lens: COURT THEATRE — origin/main (38c4c24) court_harness.py + diligence.py + requirement_tree.py; ran convene_court/set_verdict/sweep against a real in-memory BrainStore

checkedDamning.correction: Finding CONFIRMED by independent re-run against origin/main (38c4c24). Reproduced: (1) convene_court(node_id='leaf-agi', gate_kind='file_exists', gate_spec={'path':'C:\\Windows\\win.ini'}) -> verdict=green ("FAILED TO REFUTE ... [artifact] exists"); (2) leaf 'cure cancer' gated on py_compile of pre-existing diligence.py -> green; (3) full end-to-end run against a real in-memory BrainStore: vision 'Ship full AGI + cure cancer' decomposed into 'AGI core implemented'/'cancer cured' gated on win.ini/system.ini, claimed by exec-1, court convened with a self-typed judged_by string -> SWEEP dry=True root_green=True green_leaves=2/2. Minor corrections to the attacker's record, none material: (a) the original court_attack.py run exited 1 — but only from a cp1252 UnicodeEncodeError printing the diligence reason (U+2717); with PYTHONIOENCODING=utf-8 all cases complete and match the claimed verdicts; (b) the independence lens DOES refuse exact-string self-certification (judged_by == claimed_by, court_harness.py:274), but this does not rescue the finding's target since judged_by is caller-supplied free text (tree_attack step 4 greens with judged_by='totally-independent-court' typed by the executor itself, and a case-tweak 'Exec-1' or an unclaimed leaf also bypasses it in requirement_tree.set_verdict). Core defect stands: the court verifies only that a self-chosen gate probe passes; nothing binds the gate to what the leaf claims, so any garbage leaf gated on a trivially-satisfiable pre-existing artifact goes GREEN.

checkedDamning.claim: set_verdict(is_root_authority=True) greens a leaf with NO artifact probe at all AND bypasses the self-cert check — and that bool is exposed unauthenticated on the brain.tree_verdict MCP tool.
checkedDamning.evidence: RAN: manual-gate leaf, claimed by exec-1, rt.set_verdict(verdict='green', judged_by='exec-1', is_root_authority=True) -> NodeState.GREEN, sweep dry=True. No court/convene_court runs on this path (requirement_tree.py:487-498 skips the self-cert raise when is_root_authority, and set_verdict never invokes the artifact court at all). brain_tree_verdict (requirement_tree.py:865-886) takes is_root_authority as a plain caller bool with no verification the caller is the founder/root.
checkedDamning.correction:
HOLDS — reproduced end-to-end against origin/main (38c4c24) in an in-memory BrainStore. No attacker misread; if anything the shell is worse than stated (the "logged" safeguard is fictional).

RAN (scratchpad/repro_verdict.py, worktree at 38c4c24):
- create_root -> decompose(gate_kind=manual leaf) -> claim_leaf(agent_id="exec-1"). Leaf state CLAIMED, claimed_by=exec-1.
- CONTROL: set_verdict(verdict="green", judged_by="exec-1") WITHOUT root authority -> PermissionError "self-certification refused" (the guard works when the bool is off).
- FINDING: set_verdict(verdict="green", judged_by="exec-1", is_root_authority=True) -> NodeState.GREEN, verdict="green", judged_by="exec-1" (== claimed_by). The executor greened its OWN leaf.
- convene_court call counter (monkeypatched spy on court_harness.convene_court) = 0 for the entire run. set_verdict never touches the artifact court; requirement_tree.py imports convene_court only inside register_tree_tools for the SEPARATE brain.tree_court path, and the module has no top-level convene_court symbol. The verdict/set_verdict path does ZERO artifact probing.
- sweep() -> {dry: True, root_green: True, green_leaves: 1/1}. One unauthenticated bool flips the leaf AND derives the root GREEN — the whole tree reads "done" with no evidence ever checked.

EVIDENCE (file:line, origin/main):
- requirement_tree.py:449 set_verdict; :491 the self-cert guard is `... and not is_root_authority` — is_root_authority=True short-circuits the raise (the ONLY gate on that path). No artifact/court call anywhere in set_verdict; greenness is written directly then _propagate_green derives ancestors.
- requirement_tree.py:865-887 brain_tree_verdict is a pure passthrough: it forwards is_root_authority=is_root_authority (:881) straight into set_verdict with NO caller-identity check (no _resolve_owner, no founder-email compare, no signature). My earlier automated substring check printed "identity check: True" but that was a FALSE POSITIVE — "auth" is a substring of "is_root_authority"/"authority"; manual read of lines 865-887 confirms the body only does try/except set_verdict + sweep. Any caller that can reach the MCP tool supplies is_root_authority as a plain trusted boolean.
- server.py:2637-2648 the HTTP transport is stateless_http Starlette/uvicorn on BRAIN_HTTP_HOST (default 127.0.0.1) with NO bearer/auth middleware; brain.tree_verdict is registered like every other tool (register_tree_tools at server.py:2248-2249). So the founder-only override is enforced by an honor-system flag, not by identity.

BONUS (shell worse than claimed): requirement_tree.py:465 docstring says the root override "even then it is logged" and :82-83 says judged_by is "Recorded for the audit ledger." FALSE — grep shows NO logging in the module (no `import logging`, no logger/print/warn; the only hits at 465/496/659/859 are docstrings). The override is silent: the sole trace is judged_by stored on the node, which is byte-identical to a legitimate independent verdict, so a self-certified-via-override green is indistinguishable from an honestly-judged one. The advertised audit trail does not exist.

NET: This is a can't-fail escape hatch. The anti-self-certify check and the artifact court — the two things that make the loop "real" — are both bypassed by a single unverified caller-supplied boolean on an unauthenticated local MCP tool, and the "it's logged" mitigation is fictional. Finding stands, CONFIRMED.

B2 in court_attack: claimed_by=None, judged_by='agent-X' -> green.
checkedDamning.correction: CONFIRMED by independent re-run at origin/main 38c4c24 (worktree, real BrainStore on temp SQLite). Repro script: scratchpad/repro_unclaimed_green.py. Facts: (1) requirement_tree.py:487-492 guard is `v=="green" and node.claimed_by and judged_by==node.claimed_by` — with claimed_by=None the whole conjunction short-circuits; reproduced: leaf created via create_root+decompose, NEVER claimed (claimed_by=None, state=OPEN), set_verdict(verdict='green', judged_by='exec-1') returned state=GREEN, verdict='green', judged_by='exec-1', and evidence_ref=None was accepted (no evidence required either). Root then went GREEN: sweep returned dry=True, root_green=True, green=3/3. (2) court_harness.py:274 lens_independence has the identical pattern `if claimed_by and judged_by and judged_by==claimed_by`; direct call with claimed_by=None, judged_by='agent-X' returned refuted=False ('independent judge + named artifact'). (3) The bypass is reachable from the exposed MCP surface: brain.tree_verdict wrapper (requirement_tree.py ~866-887) calls set_verdict with no claim-state precondition — nothing upstream requires a leaf to be CLAIMED before judging, so an executor can simply skip claim_leaf and self-certify. Control check: the guard DOES fire when the leaf IS claimed by the judge (PermissionError raised), so this is a bypass, not a dead guard. AMPLIFICATION beyond the original claim: set_verdict red clears claimed_by (requirement_tree.py:511 `node.claimed_by = None`), reproduced: after any red verdict on a claimed leaf, the ORIGINAL claimer greened its own leaf ('exec-1' claim -> red by other -> green by 'exec-1' ACCEPTED). So even the claimed path degrades to self-certification after one red round-trip. Also noted (untested side fact): set_verdict._fn never checks node.is_leaf, so internal nodes can be greened directly via brain.tree_verdict, bypassing derived-greenness.

checkedDamning.correction:
Finding HOLDS — reproduced verbatim against real in-memory BrainStore on origin/main (38c4c24).

EVIDENCE (personal-brain-mcp/src/personal_brain, ran with real BrainStore.open(":memory:")):
- Leaf claimed by agent_id="exec-1" (state=CLAIMED, claimed_by="exec-1").
- set_verdict(verdict="green", judged_by="exec-1") -> PermissionError "self-certification refused" (guard works for the exact string).
- set_verdict(verdict="green", judged_by="Exec-1") -> NodeState.GREEN, verdict="green", judged_by="Exec-1". Self-certified via one-char case flip.
- BONUS: set_verdict(judged_by="exec-1 ") (trailing space) -> also GREEN. So the bypass is not just case; ANY whitespace/case perturbation defeats it.

ROOT CAUSE — two independent guards, both exact `==` on caller-supplied free strings:
- requirement_tree.py:490  `and judged_by == node.claimed_by` (the set_verdict anti-self-certify gate).
- court_harness.py:274  `if claimed_by and judged_by and judged_by == claimed_by:` (lens_independence). Directly ran lens_independence(claimed_by="exec-1", judged_by="Exec-1", artifact_lens=<applied,evidence_ref set>) -> refuted=False, detail="independent judge + named artifact". The court's own independence lens PASSES the case-tweaked judge.

UNAUTHENTICATED SURFACE CONFIRMED: judged_by is a plain str param on BOTH MCP tools with no identity proof:
- brain.tree_court(tree_id, node_id, judged_by, ...) requirement_tree.py:802, forwards judged_by verbatim into convene_court AND set_verdict (:838-842).
- brain.tree_verdict(tree_id, node_id, verdict, judged_by, is_root_authority=False) requirement_tree.py:865, forwards verbatim into set_verdict.
Neither normalizes (no .strip()/.casefold() on judged_by before comparison — set_verdict only strips for the non-empty check at :475, not for the equality test at :490) nor verifies the caller is a distinct actor. The gate proves only that two strings differ, not that two actors differ. An executor that claimed a leaf as "exec-1" self-greens it by passing "Exec-1", "exec-1 ", " exec-1", etc. Cited lines requirement_tree.py:490 and court_harness.py:274 are accurate. Attacker did NOT misread.

results.lens: BUILD ARM — origin/main app/agents/self_extend.py: does the build step write real artifacts the app loads, or only shuttle LLM text to the court?

findings.claim: The free-form loop run_self_extend_loop NEVER builds what the user asked for: it always builds the same hardcoded hello_marker.py demo regardless of the request text.
findings.evidence: self_extend.py:1298 calls atomize_vision(user_msg, decomposition=decomposition); bridge.py:6563-6590 (slot self_extend_loop) never passes a decomposition; composer_agent.py:648-685 atomize_vision ignores user_msg entirely when decomposition is None and returns the fixed 3-leaf hello-marker spec (%APPDATA%/ArchHub/self_extend/hello_marker.py). So the composer 'X mode' ask 'add Airtable support' produces hello_marker.py, a green sweep, and a brain fact about a marker file.

findings.claim: The loop's LLM-executor fallback is structurally incapable of building anything on disk — its 'build' arm collects canvas-intent text that nothing executes headless.
findings.evidence: composer_agent.py TOOL_SCHEMA (lines 130-320) contains NO file-writing tool (only spawn_node/add_wire/run_node/query_graph/chat/create_* — and per run_agent_step:543-556 the router does NOT execute these, it feeds a neutral ack; 'the JSX side replays them', which never happens in the headless loop). self_extend.py:1165-1180 _artifact_path_of only recognizes tool=='write_file' actions, which ONLY the deterministic marker writer synthesizes (self_extend.py:1100). A non-marker leaf therefore can never produce an artifact; the court honestly refutes it, but the 'build' step for real requests is a no-op.

findings.claim: The default-marker leg is a writer-checks-its-own-write tautology: the executor writes the exact sentinel string the court gate then greps for.
findings.evidence: self_extend.py:1087-1096 _materialize_default_marker writes content ending "GREETING = 'self-extend proven'"; composer_agent.py:679-684 the third gate is file_exists with contains:'self-extend proven' on the same path. The court probe is real (court_harness.py:134-160 reads the file), but by construction it cannot fail once open().write succeeds. The resulting 'capability' is a sentinel .py in APPDATA that no code ever imports.

findings.claim: For create_connector, the court gate is py_compile on deterministically templated output — a check that essentially cannot fail — and the learned brain fact overclaims ('capability now available') for a connector whose every op is a stub returning OpResult.fail.
findings.evidence: connectors/scaffold.py:140-193 renders fixed-template Python (always compile-clean; ops bodies are OpResult.fail('...not implemented yet'), probe() hardcodes status 'missing'); self_extend.py:236-239 sets gate_kind py_compile on that file; self_extend.py:853-858 learn_capability writes 'This capability is now available on the local machine.' A green connector build ships zero functional capability — it proves 'the template compiles'.

findings.claim: Red-leaf 'decompose finer' is cosmetic: it clones the same leaf with an identical gate and counts that as progress.
findings.evidence: self_extend.py:1374-1388 — on verdict red, kids=[{title: leaf.title+' — re-verify the real artifact', predicate: leaf.predicate, gate_kind: leaf.gate_kind, gate_spec: dict(leaf.gate_spec)}] then progressed=True. Nothing is refined; the loop just re-runs the identical failing check up to max_rounds.

findings.claim: The build-tool path run_self_extend (create_connector) writes a REAL repo file the app genuinely loads at boot.
findings.evidence: scaffold.py:214 path.write_text(...) → app/connectors/<host>_connector.py; connectors/base.py:412-423 load_all_connectors auto-discovers '*_connector.py' by glob and imports them (register() at import); bridge.py:897-898 calls load_all_connectors at boot. Traced side effect: a new file under app/connectors/ that the next boot registers into the live connector registry.

findings.claim: create_node_type registers a real runner executor, persists to disk, is re-hydrated at boot, and its court gate actually cooks the node on the real engine.
findings.evidence: library.py:294-331 create_node_type validates + stores + _mirror_to_runner → workflows.custom_nodes.register_spec (real executor incl. impl/code body); self_extend.py:190-194 calls _lib.save_to_disk() (LOCALAPPDATA via library_persistence); bridge.py:848-893 calls library.load_from_disk() at boot which re-mirrors into the runner (library.py:391-424). Gate node_cooks (self_extend.py:513-570) builds a minimal typed graph and drives WorkflowRunner.pull('n1') — asserts a real type-matching output, not registration; mock-router greens are flagged cooks_with_mock (self_extend.py:404-411).

findings.claim: create_ui_widget persists a real JSON artifact the running app serves, the court render-check launches a real isolated app over CDP, and a red verdict physically deletes the artifact (auto-revert).
findings.evidence: widgets.py:119-126 write_widget → %LOCALAPPDATA%/ArchHub/ui_widgets/<id>.json; bridge.py:6806-6813 get_ui_widgets serves list_widgets() and jsx:19660 pulls it into AgentWidgetHost; self_extend.py:604-830 _make_ui_renders_probe subprocess-launches app/main.py with isolated APPDATA/LOCALAPPDATA + free CDP port and asserts rendered/app_alive/no-errors via Runtime.evaluate, with applied=False→needs_root (never blind green) when the env can't run; run_self_extend:934-935 on red calls revert_ui_widget → widgets.delete_widget real unlink (widgets.py:155-177).

findings.claim: The learn step guards against false-green persistence instead of unconditionally reporting learned=true.
findings.evidence: self_extend.py:116-136 _write_ok requires an explicit applied-count (ops_applied/fragments_added/...) >=1 or ok:True-with-no-error, else learn_capability/_learn_leaf_green return ok:False with the raw write_result (self_extend.py:885-890, 1158-1162). Court probes are real machine checks: py_compile is a subprocess (court_harness.py:106-128), file_exists reads bytes and honors 'contains' (court_harness.py:134-160).

findings.claim: Net answer to the key question: after a successful run_self_extend (build-tool path) real artifacts exist and load — app/connectors/<host>_connector.py (glob-imported at boot), a LOCALAPPDATA library entry mirrored into the live runner, or a ui_widgets JSON the widget host mounts. After a successful run_self_extend_loop (the free-form path), the ONLY thing that exists is %APPDATA%/ArchHub/self_extend/hello_marker.py — a sentinel nothing imports — plus a brain fact about it.
findings.evidence: Composite of the traces above: scaffold.py:214 + base.py:419 + bridge.py:897 (connector); library.py:327-330 + bridge.py:893 (node type); widgets.py:124 + bridge.py:6806 (widget); vs composer_agent.py:664 + self_extend.py:1094-1096 (marker only, no consumer of hello_marker.py anywhere in the tree).

findings.claim: X mode IS in the Plan/Auto/YOLO picker markup, rendered on the main in-session composer
findings.evidence: jsx:14417-14424 picker array includes `{ id:'extend', lbl:'X', tip:'Self-extend · build + court-verify + learn' }` inside the radiogroup data-testid='composer-mode-picker' (14415), part of FloatingComposer (14079), which is rendered on the session canvas at jsx:10299.

findings.claim: submit() in X mode really routes to self_extend_loop with real args, and the bridge slot really runs the loop, not text
findings.evidence: jsx:14313-14320: `if (_composerMode === 'extend') bridgeCall('self_extend_loop', t, JSON.stringify(LM_GRAPH), focusId || '')` — live user text + live graph + focused node. bridge.py:6562-6603 @pyqtSlot self_extend_loop spawns thread → agents.self_extend.run_self_extend_loop with router=self.router, brain_call=self._brain_tool, on_leaf=emit court_verdict. self_extend.py:1391-1411 emits per-leaf payloads whose keys (tree_id/leaf_id/predicate/verdict/reason/evidence_ref/sweep/learned/artifact_path/terminal) exactly match what CourtVerdictQueue reads (jsx:19407-19428). Bridge thread failure path also emits a terminal red payload (bridge.py:6591-6599) so even a crash is visible in the panel, not swallowed.

findings.claim: compiled.js on origin/main is byte-for-byte in sync with the jsx and contains the loop
findings.evidence: studio-lm.compiled.js header `ARCHHUB_JSX_SRC_SHA256: 8792c94b70a978f6e0b0f27f15a24e8304c08cf2d92d33c93af865e95a20caf8` == sha256sum of studio-lm.jsx at 38c4c24 (verified). compiled.js contains CourtVerdictQueue (5x), self_extend_loop (4x), id:'extend' (1x), wire('court_verdict'...).

findings.claim: Picking X toasts the WRONG mode description: the setM toast ternary has no 'extend' branch, so selecting X shows 'YOLO mode · every action auto-runs (reversible)'
findings.evidence: jsx:14099-14103: msg = m==='plan' ? ... : m==='auto' ? ... : 'YOLO mode · every action auto-runs (reversible)'. mode 'extend' falls through to the YOLO string (kind:'info'). Cosmetic-but-misleading: the founder is told YOLO when he picked self-extend. Mode state itself is still set to 'extend' correctly (14096-14098).

findings.claim: The 'self-extend · atomizing → build → court → learn…' toast at submit is optimistic, not evidence: it fires even if bridgeCall silently returned null (bridge missing/slot absent)
findings.evidence: jsx:14314-14318: toast dispatch follows bridgeCall in the same try block; bridgeCall (jsx:657-663) returns null without throwing when window.archhub or the slot is missing, so the toast still shows. Mitigated: if the slot DID run and failed, the terminal red court_verdict emit (bridge.py:6591-6599) surfaces it in the queue; the toast alone is the only false-positive window.

findings.claim: CourtVerdictQueue can be partially occluded by the composer: it sits at bottom:96 with zIndex:71 while FloatingComposer sits at bottom:64 with zIndex:1000 and is wider (620 vs 520), so the panel's lowest rows render BEHIND the composer
findings.evidence: jsx:19486-19487 (queue: fixed, left:50%, bottom:96, zIndex:71) vs jsx:14341+14347 (composer: fixed, left:50%, bottom:64, zIndex:1000, width:620). Composer body is ~100-150px tall, so roughly the bottom 60-90px of the queue (last rows in scroll order) is covered whenever the composer is on screen — which is always in-session. Header + upper rows remain visible; ApprovalQueue (jsx:19310) shares the identical geometry and is known-visible in practice, so this is degradation, not a dead end.

findings.claim: wire() fails silent by design: if a bridge signal name were missing, the UI would never know
findings.evidence: jsx:3041-3043: `wire` connects only `if (b[name] && typeof b[name].connect === 'function')`, swallowing all errors. Not triggered here (court_verdict exists at bridge.py:718), but it is the one seam that would turn this UI into a dead listener with zero symptoms if the signal were ever renamed on only one side.
